name: Agency
short_definition: The capacity to select and execute actions that influence future states according to an objective.
why_it_matters: Agency is central to the book because a capable system becomes more dangerous when it can act, adapt, and persist toward objectives.
my_position: Agency should be separated from intelligence, cognition, and sentience. A system can be highly capable without being strongly agentic.
reni_connection: This connects to your lever framing: agency is about what levers are available and whether the system can pull them effectively.

name: Instrumental Convergence
short_definition: The idea that many different goals lead to similar intermediate strategies, like acquiring resources, preserving operation, or gaining information.
why_it_matters: It explains why a misaligned AI does not need to hate humans to become dangerous.
my_position: The concept is compelling, but the strength depends on whether the system has persistent objectives.
reni_connection: This connects to your focus on chips, energy, infrastructure, and institutional protection.

name: Grey Box
short_definition: A system whose internal mechanisms are inspectable but not fully interpretable.
why_it_matters: This is a better description of LLMs than 'black box.' We can inspect weights and activations, but we cannot fully explain all behavior.
my_position: LLMs are grey boxes, not black boxes. Understanding is incomplete, not absent.
reni_connection: This came from the genetics/DNA analogy discussion.
